name: Milsaps Bus Lines
principals: Joe Milsaps (founder, deceased 2012); successor family operator listed as Joe Milsaps in current records
districts_or_customers: Knox County Schools (TN) - one of multiple small independent contractors
recent_news: Original principal Joe Milsaps (Knox County school bus contractor and retired KFD Captain) died in 2012; business apparently continues at 9305 Dutchtown Rd under Milsaps family.
fit_notes: Very small (1-3 bus) Knox County contractor with succession event in founder family - likely too small for direct Summit tuck-in but could be route bolt-on if Summit builds Knoxville scale.

name: Jwbeard School Bus Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely Worcester County Public Schools (Berlin/Ocean City area, MD)
recent_news: No public news, web presence, or operational details located; address is a residential-style location (Sweet As Sugar Ln, Berlin MD).
fit_notes: Single-bus owner-operator in Worcester County MD - too small to be a meaningful Summit tuck-in on its own; only relevant as part of a Worcester County rollup.

name: H M Ramirez Bus Co Inc
districts_or_customers: Listed on NM PED School Bus Contractor List - likely serves Hobbs Municipal Schools / Lea County area
recent_news: Appears on NM Public Education Department school bus contractor list; confirmed Hobbs operation under Hector Ramirez as owner.
fit_notes: Tiny owner-operator in southeastern NM (Hobbs/Lea County); NM is outside Summit's existing core footprint and scale is too small for a standalone tuck-in.

name: Paige Bus Svc LLC
districts_or_customers: FMCSA-authorized for-hire passenger carrier; appears to be charter rather than contracted school routes (no confirmed Hampton City Schools contract)
recent_news: DOT #4192707, 1 power unit, 1 driver; established 2009; appears to operate as charter/for-hire rather than dedicated school-district contractor.
fit_notes: Single-unit charter operator, not a school-district contractor in the Summit sense - NOT a fit for Summit student-transportation tuck-in strategy.

name: Metcalf Bus Svc
principals: Roger 'Skeeter' Metcalf (took over from his father 1969, died April 2015)
districts_or_customers: Dilworth-Glyndon-Felton School District (ISD 2164) - Roger Metcalf ran buses for DGF for 42 years per obituary
recent_news: Founder/operator Roger 'Skeeter' Metcalf died April 25, 2015 at age 76 after 42 years running buses for DGF schools; uncertain whether business continued under family or wound down post-2015.
fit_notes: Possibly dormant/wound-down post-2015; even if active, sub-scale 1-3 bus operation in DGF district - not a meaningful standalone Summit tuck-in target.
